title: Tender Smoky Greek Lamb Kebabs
description: Grassy and snappy, these kebabs feature dark brown soft sugar and are cooked to perfection over binchotan charcoal, making them a great monsoon Greek dish. The combination of flavors and textures is sure to delight.
instructions: Preheat the binchotan charcoal grill to medium high heat.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
In a small bowl, mix together the dark brown soft sugar, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Brush the sugar mixture evenly onto both sides of the lamb cubes.
Place the skewers onto the preheated grill and cook for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally.
Check the lamb for doneness, it should reach an internal temperature of 145 degrees.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or oregano if desired.
